tldr: Move video workloads from Cloudinary's general media platform to Mux, a specialized video infrastructure API for adaptive streaming and analytics.
why_switch: Purpose-built video pipeline (adaptive bitrate, HLS, real-time data) vs Cloudinary's general media tooling
Stronger streaming performance and quality analytics for video-heavy products
Cleaner developer ergonomics for video upload, processing, and playback
Better tooling for live streaming if you need it
Predictable per-minute pricing model for delivery
what_to_export: Public IDs / paths and folder structure
Tags and metadata associated with videos
Currently used video URLs and embeds (player references)
Captions, subtitles, and other text tracks
export_method: Use Cloudinary's Admin API or CLI to enumerate video resources and download masters in batches. Capture metadata, captions, and thumbnails alongside each file in a migration manifest. For very large libraries, request bulk export via Cloudinary support. Refer to Cloudinary's official docs for current procedures.
import_method: Create a Mux account and use the Mux Video API to ingest masters as new Assets — Mux can pull from a public URL or you can upload via their direct upload flow. Mux generates playback IDs and HLS streams. Replace player embeds with a Mux-compatible player (mux-player or any HLS player) and update database references from Cloudinary public IDs to Mux playback IDs. Consult Mux's official docs for current ingest options.

concept_mapping.note: Map old IDs to new IDs in your DB
Adaptive bitrate is handled automatically by Mux
Use Mux signing keys per their docs
Attach via API after asset creation

gotchas: Mux is video-only — image assets still need a separate solution
Player swap requires QA across browsers and devices
Re-encoding all videos through Mux can take significant time for large libraries
Pricing is per delivered minute — model your traffic carefully before committing
Old Cloudinary video URLs may be cached in CDNs/clients; plan redirects if needed
Verify DRM/access control parity if you rely on Cloudinary's restrictions

email.subject: Cancelling Cloudinary subscription — moving video to Mux
email.body:
Hi Cloudinary team,

We're moving our video infrastructure to Mux. Please let me know how to cancel our Cloudinary subscription at the end of the current billing cycle and confirm the data retention timeline after closure.

Could you also share the recommended approach for a bulk export of our master video files via the Admin API?

Thanks for the service.

Best regards
